content: Just as the human educational use of reflexion (as taught in high school literacy curricula) requires stepping outside one's own immediate reasoning to evaluate thought processes from an external, structured perspective, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to mirror this scaffolded educational approach, rather than relying on unguided spontaneous self-evaluation. This means framing LLM self-reflection as a graded literacy exercise rather than a purely technical debugging step, where the model first learns to articulate its own reasoning like a student writing a reflection essay, before identifying inconsistencies across perspectives.
